title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Elijah and Ellery discuss speaking with Mrs. Bradwood before being interrupted by a detective. The detective informs Officer Vorn that Paul Roman and the Egyptologist Strick are at the docks requesting a meeting. Jonah Lincoln, visibly angry, asks to accompany the officer. On their way to the docks, they pass Dr. Temple, who is preoccupied with a black bag.

At the docks, Roman and a shivering Strick demand to be released, claiming the police have locked their guests on Oyster Island and ruined their business. The "madman" Striker interrupts with religious outbursts, which Professor Yadley dismisses as insanity. When Vorn refuses to let them leave, Roman becomes fierce but remains restrained. Vorn questions Roman regarding his whereabouts on the night Thomas was killed. Roman proposes that reliable witnesses could clear his suspicion.

As Roman begins to speak, Jonah Lincoln becomes extremely agitated and threatens to kill Roman. Roman eventually reveals his alibi: from 10:30 to 11:30 on the night of the murder, he was on a date with Mrs. Bradley, meeting her at Bridewood Pier to spend time by the seashore on Oyster Island. Vorn instructs Roman and Strick to return to the island while he investigates the claim.

Following the revelation, Jonah attacks Roman, citing insults to his sister and Roman's influence over Mrs. Bradley. Roman retaliates with a powerful uppercut that knocks Jonah unconscious. Roman then jumps into Strick's motorboat and flees toward Oyster Island, pursued by Vorn in a patrol boat. Later, the group gathers in a colonial-era house, where a recovering Jonah sits in a rocking chair while Ellery, Escham, and Professor Yadley smoke in silence.
